We're not here to replace your product. We're the missing layer that makes your product defensible in the long run.

The entire Careerspan thesis rests on the premise that behavioral signal (how people think) is more valuable than profile data (what they've done). This challenges the fundamental premise of LinkedIn and traditional resumes.

Handshake has 1,600 universities but no way to capture behavioral signal at scale. Careerspan has the coaching infrastructure to capture signal but lacks distribution. The combination creates a data moat neither could build alone.

Positioning Careerspan as a strategic hedge for Handshake's declining core business ($200M, only 6% growth). The insight: even if the partnership never becomes a major revenue line, it provides data supremacy and option value.

The repeated emphasis on leveraging founder club networks and warm introductions reveals an implicit strategy: careerspan is playing a relationship-heavy, founder-to-founder game rather than a traditional enterprise sales motion.

We're a three-person team. Like we're not even that expensive essentially to like to bring on. Emphasizing the 3-person team as an asset (low acquisition overhead) turns a potential weakness into strength.
Thread the needle of being opportunistic but not just showing them as an opportunity, us not being desperate. Active discussion of Handshake as potential acquisition/partnership target.

The emphasis on month-on-month retention and return rates reveals a long-term data play. The real value isn't in helping someone get one job—it's in tracking their entire career trajectory and predicting future success.
